question.label: Next-step focus
question.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?
question.description: Closes by turning the conversation into a concrete next move without opening a new thread.

answer: I want to re-engage more visibly, but I need a steadier footing first.

assessment.note: She names a direction to re-engage and a prerequisite of steadier footing, which shows some clarity on her immediate focus.

new_queue.label: Priority ranking
new_queue.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?

issues: ref_alias q_next_step_focus_9 not in remaining queue — treating as new

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
What would help most is clearer prioritization and a little more air cover in cross-team decisions.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.
What would help most is clearer prioritization and a little more air cover in cross-team decisions.
I want to re-engage more visibly, but I need a steadier footing first.
